item.title:
New research result: we use Claude to make fully autonomous progress on scalable oversight research, as measured by performance gap recovered (PGR).
Claude iterates on a number of different technique
item.description:
New research result: we use Claude to make fully autonomous progress on scalable oversight research, as measured by performance gap recovered (PGR).
Claude iterates on a number of different techniques and ends up significantly outperforming human researchers for $18k in credits. https://t.co/fbVpCP

item.title: RT @jachiam0: At what is possibly a risk to my whole career I will say: this doesn't seem great. Lately I have been describing my role as s…

item.title:
Sonnet 4.5 is out!
It’s the most aligned frontier model yet; a lot of progress relative to Sonnet 4 and Opus 4.1! https://t.co/w5cRNcR3ma
item.description:
Sonnet 4.5 is out!
It’s the most aligned frontier model yet; a lot of progress relative to Sonnet 4 and Opus 4.1! https://t.co/w5cRNcR3ma

item.title: RT @Jack_W_Lindsey: We're launching an "AI psychiatry" team as part of interpretability efforts at Anthropic! We'll be researching phenome…
item.description: RT @Jack_W_Lindsey: We're launching an "AI psychiatry" team as part of interpretability efforts at Anthropic! We'll be researching phenome…

item.title:
In March we published a paper on alignment audits: teams of humans were tasked to find the problems in model we trained to be misaligned.
Now we have agents that can do it automatically 42% of the ti
item.description:
In March we published a paper on alignment audits: teams of humans were tasked to find the problems in model we trained to be misaligned.
Now we have agents that can do it automatically 42% of the time.

item.title:
If you don't train your CoTs to look nice, you could get some safety from monitoring them.
This seems good to do!
But I'm skeptical this will work reliably enough to be load-bearing in a safety case
item.description:
If you don't train your CoTs to look nice, you could get some safety from monitoring them.
This seems good to do!
But I'm skeptical this will work reliably enough to be load-bearing in a safety case.
Plus as RL is scaled up, I expect CoTs to become less and less legible.

item.title: RT @sleepinyourhat: 🧵✨🙏 With the new Claude Opus 4, we conducted what I think is by far the most thorough pre-launch alignment assessment t…
item.description: RT @sleepinyourhat: 🧵✨🙏 With the new Claude Opus 4, we conducted what I think is by far the most thorough pre-launch alignment assessment t…

item.title: So many things to love about Claude 4! My favorite is that the model is so strong that we had to turn on additional safety mitigations according to Anthropic's responsible scaling policy
item.description: So many things to love about Claude 4! My favorite is that the model is so strong that we had to turn on additional safety mitigations according to Anthropic's responsible scaling policy

item.title:
RT @yanda_chen_: My first paper @AnthropicAI is out!
We show that Chains-of-Thought often don’t reflect models’ true reasoning—posing chal…
item.description:
RT @yanda_chen_: My first paper @AnthropicAI is out!
We show that Chains-of-Thought often don’t reflect models’ true reasoning—posing chal…

item.title: Somewhat surprising that faithfulness of chain-of-thought doesn't improve much with outcome-based RL
item.description: Somewhat surprising that faithfulness of chain-of-thought doesn't improve much with outcome-based RL
